macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US stocks slump as inflation fears and geopolitical uncertainty hit Wall Street
items.summary: The Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq all fell sharply in the latest session, with investors reacting to renewed inflation worries and uncertainty tied to the Iran war. Nvidia and Amazon were among the notable stock declines mentioned in the selloff.

items.title: Fed leadership transition: Kevin Warsh takes over as Federal Reserve chair
items.summary: Kevin Warsh has taken over at the Federal Reserve after being confirmed by the Senate, marking a major leadership change at the central bank. Markets are closely watching what his arrival could mean for future policy direction.

items.title: Powell’s Fed legacy and the challenge of inflation come into focus
items.summary: CBS News highlighted Jerome Powell’s record as he exits as Fed chair, noting that his tenure was shaped by the pandemic, supply shocks, and the sharp post-COVID inflation surge. The piece also emphasizes that inflation remains above the Fed’s 2% target, keeping policy pressure elevated.

items.title: Economic calendar points to key U.S. data and Fed events ahead
items.summary: Trading Economics lists several near-term U.S. macro releases and Fed-related events, including pending home sales, Treasury auctions, FOMC minutes, and a Federal Reserve speech. These data points are likely to shape expectations for rates and growth.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI launches $4 billion enterprise deployment and consulting business
items.summary: OpenAI is creating a new unit, OpenAI Deployment Company, to speed enterprise AI adoption through embedded engineering teams and consulting services. The effort is backed by more than $4 billion in initial investment and targets high-impact business use cases.

items.title: Google reportedly developing Gemini Omni AI video generation system
items.summary: Leaks suggest Google is working on a new Gemini-based video generation tool called Omni ahead of Google I/O 2026. Early demos reportedly show conversational video generation, remixing, and editing with improved realism and text rendering.

items.title: Sakana AI unveils reinforcement-learning model to orchestrate multiple frontier models
items.summary: Sakana AI introduced RL Conductor, a 7B-parameter orchestration model trained with reinforcement learning to route tasks across multiple AI systems. The model dynamically coordinates among frontier and open-source models instead of relying on fixed workflows.

items.title: HubSpot releases free AI visibility dashboard as ChatGPT referrals decline
items.summary: HubSpot launched AEO Sensor, a public dashboard that tracks citations, volatility, and referral patterns across answer engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity. The release follows HubSpot data showing ChatGPT generated its lowest business referral traffic in 12 months in April 2026.

items.title: Google DeepMind shows experimental AI-powered mouse cursor interactions
items.summary: Google DeepMind demonstrated early concepts for an AI-enhanced mouse cursor that uses natural-language commands, gestures, and contextual awareness. The demos included tasks like moving recipe ingredients into shopping lists and helping complete restaurant reservations from video content.

items.title: U.S. government to evaluate upcoming AI models for national security risks before release
items.summary: NIST announced a new multi-agency task force will assess cutting-edge AI models for cybersecurity, biosecurity, and chemical weapons risks before public deployment. The move marks a major shift toward pre-release model evaluation and could lead to an executive order requiring approval before deployment.

items.title: Anthropic overtakes OpenAI in enterprise usage, according to Ramp data
items.summary: A new report highlighted in Newcomer says Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI in enterprise usage for the first time. The development underscores intensifying competition among frontier model providers for corporate adoption.

items.title: AI investment wave drives big funding and IPO activity across the sector
items.summary: Newcomer reports that the AI trade continues to fuel major market gains, including the Cerebras IPO and substantial funding rounds for Anduril, Isomorphic Labs, and Recursive Superintelligence. The piece also notes continued strength in AI-linked chip stocks and broader market indexes.

items.title: AI reshaping retinal disease research, international study finds
items.summary: A bibliometric analysis suggests AI has become a central force in retinal diagnostics, with rapid growth in deep learning, OCT imaging, and diabetic retinopathy research since 2015. The study also points to rising interest in explainable AI, telemedicine, and multimodal imaging.

items.title: Entry-level productivity expectations rise as AI changes hiring patterns
items.summary: A D2L and Morning Consult report found nearly half of U.S. HR leaders say AI is raising productivity expectations for entry-level employees. Some companies are hiring fewer junior staff and more mid-level workers as AI automates tasks once assigned to early-career employees.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Subnautica 2 launches to huge player counts, with 2 million sales in 12 hours
items.summary: Subnautica 2 reportedly sold 2 million copies in its first 12 hours and peaked at more than 600,000 concurrent players, making it one of the biggest launches in recent memory. The strong debut has immediately put the survival sequel among the most notable gaming stories of the day.

items.title: Best Buy affiliate email sparks GTA 6 pre-order speculation
items.summary: Best Buy reportedly told affiliates that Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders will start next week, leading to fresh speculation that Rockstar or Take-Two could be preparing a new trailer or marketing beat. The timing has drawn attention because Take-Two’s next financial results are also due soon.

items.title: Nintendo adds more Virtual Boy games to Switch Online
items.summary: Nintendo has already added most of the Virtual Boy library to Switch Online following its latest update, with five new titles available. The move is one of the more unusual retro-library expansions Nintendo has made recently.

items.title: Lego 2K Drive will be delisted from digital storefronts on May 19
items.summary: 2K Drive is being removed from digital storefronts three years to the day after release, although its multiplayer servers will remain online for another year. The delisting has been highlighted as a notable end-of-sale development for a major licensed racing game.

items.title: Bungie outlines plans to expand Marathon with duo queues and more PvE
items.summary: Bungie says it is planning new matchmaking options, additional PvE content, and better onboarding for Marathon as it looks to strengthen the extraction shooter’s long-term future. The update signals continued iteration after player feedback.

items.title: Moss and Moss Book II are heading to consoles
items.summary: The cute VR platformers Moss and Moss Book II are being bundled together for console release this summer, along with some DLC. The move brings the well-reviewed VR series to a much wider audience.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: OpenAI launches personal finance tools for ChatGPT Pro users in the U.S.
items.summary: OpenAI introduced preview personal finance features for U.S. ChatGPT Pro subscribers, including spending analysis through a Plaid integration with access to more than 12,000 financial institutions. The rollout marks a notable expansion of ChatGPT into consumer financial services.

items.title: xAI debuts Grok Build, its first coding and workflow automation agent
items.summary: xAI launched Grok Build, an early-beta agent and CLI aimed at coding, app creation, and workflow automation. The release is initially limited to SuperGrok Heavy subscribers as the company tries to compete more directly in developer tools.
items.source: The New York Times via Techmeme

items.title: Meta brings third-party apps and games to Ray-Ban Display glasses
items.summary: Meta is expanding the capabilities of its smart glasses by adding third-party apps and games to the Ray-Ban Display line. The update could make the $800 glasses more useful beyond basic capture and display functions.

items.title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon team up to reduce mobile dead zones
items.summary: The three major U.S. carriers are joining forces to improve satellite capacity and expand coverage in underserved areas. The collaboration is aimed at tackling persistent connectivity gaps across the country.

items.title: YouTube expands AI likeness detection to all users over 18
items.summary: YouTube is broadening access to its AI likeness detection tool after earlier testing with select creators, politicians, and journalists. The feature is intended to help identify unauthorized synthetic uses of a person’s face or voice.

items.title: Researchers say Anthropic’s Mythos helped uncover macOS security vulnerabilities
items.summary: Security researchers, with help from Anthropic’s Mythos model, reportedly found vulnerabilities in macOS and other systems. The development underscores how advanced AI tools are increasingly being used in cybersecurity research.
